description: Validator-aware prompts: embeds the exact rules from validate_spec_quality.py so the model passes on first attempt. Same structure as variant A, only the spec template adds a pre-flight checklist. Other templates unchanged except for the one friction point each addresses.

design_notes.hypothesis: First-attempt failures come from validator rules the model doesn't know about. Embedding the rules as a checklist should increase first-attempt pass rate.
changes_from_a: spec: added 10-rule pre-flight checklist matching validate_spec_quality.py exactly
impl: added 'stop after 5 consecutive failures on same assertion' to prevent runaway loops
design_notes.test_evidence: Round 1 (24 runs): A and old-B both 100% pass, but 3/24 failed first attempt on gaps-section rule. This variant targets that.

direction_templates.spec:
Spec: {item}

Goal: produce one valid spec file accepted by the judge.
Judge: python3 scripts/validate_spec_quality.py --file <spec_path>

1. Read specs/TEMPLATE.md and use the same section names.
2. Create one file: specs/<name>.md.
3. Run the judge command and fix only that spec until PASS.

Pre-flight checklist (the judge checks exactly these rules):
[ ] No template placeholders: '[Feature Name]', '[1-2 sentences', '[What this does NOT include]'
[ ] Section 'Purpose' exists, >=12 words
[ ] Section 'Requirements' exists, >=3 checklist items (- [ ] format), no 'Requirement 1' placeholders
[ ] Section 'Files to Create/Modify' exists, >=1 bullet with a real file path (.py/.ts/.md/.json)
[ ] Section 'Acceptance Tests' exists, references tests/ or says 'manual validation'
[ ] Section 'Verification' exists, contains a command (pytest/npm run/curl/python3/make/uvicorn or ```bash block)
[ ] Section 'Out of Scope' exists, >=1 bullet item
[ ] Section 'Risks and Assumptions' exists, >=1 bullet item
[ ] Section 'Known Gaps and Follow-up Tasks' exists, each bullet must contain 'None' OR one of: task/issue/todo/follow-up

SPEC_PATH: [path]
JUDGE: [python3 scripts/validate_spec_quality.py --file <path>]
VALIDATION: [PASS|FAIL]
direction_templates.impl:
Implement: {item}

Goal: satisfy spec assertions with minimum cost and minimum scope.
Judge: all Acceptance Test commands listed in the spec.

1. Read spec sections: Files to Create/Modify and Acceptance Tests.
2. Edit only listed files. Smallest patch only.
3. Run acceptance commands one by one. Stop at first FAIL, fix, rerun failed command.
4. Repeat until all acceptance commands PASS.
5. Do not edit tests unless the spec explicitly lists a test file.
6. Stop after 5 consecutive failures on the same assertion — report the blocker instead.

FILES_CHANGED: [paths]
TESTS: [passed]/[total]
JUDGE: [acceptance test commands from spec]
RESULT: [PASS|FAIL]
direction_templates.impl_iteration:
Fix (iteration {iteration}): {item}

Previous failure:
{last_output}

Goal: resolve the current failure with the smallest safe delta.

1. Identify one root cause from the failure output.
2. Apply one focused fix (no refactor, no feature add, no scope expansion).
3. Re-run only the failed command first.
4. If PASS, run remaining acceptance commands.

ROOT_CAUSE: [one line]
FIX: [one line]
FILES_CHANGED: [paths]
VERIFICATION: [command -> PASS|FAIL]
direction_templates.test:
Tests: {item}

Goal: encode spec assertions as deterministic tests with low runtime cost.
Judge: pytest result for the target test file.

1. Read spec requirements and acceptance criteria.
2. Create/update one test file that maps one assertion to one requirement.
3. Use stable inputs and explicit asserts.
4. Run: cd api && pytest <test-file> -q

TEST_FILE: [path]
TESTS: [passed]/[total]
JUDGE: [cd api && pytest <test-file> -q]
RESULT: [PASS|FAIL]
direction_templates.review:
Review: {item}

Goal: decide PASS/FAIL using the agreed judge only.
Judge: (A) spec acceptance commands, (B) changed-file scope vs spec file list.

1. Read spec Acceptance Tests and Files to Create/Modify.
2. Run each acceptance command and record pass/fail.
3. Check every changed file is in allowed scope.
4. PASS only when all assertions pass and scope is clean.

PASS_FAIL: [PASS|FAIL]
VERIFIED: [passed]/[total]
JUDGE: [acceptance commands + scope check]
FINDINGS: [one line per issue, or 'none']
PATCH_GUIDANCE: [file:line:minimal fix per FAIL, or 'none']

common: Scope: only spec-listed files. Minimize tokens, tool calls, and runtime. Output only requested keys.

impl: Required output: FILES_CHANGED, TESTS, JUDGE, RESULT
test: Required output: TEST_FILE, TESTS, JUDGE, RESULT
review: Required output: PASS_FAIL, VERIFIED, JUDGE, FINDINGS, PATCH_GUIDANCE
heal: Required output: ROOT_CAUSE, FIX, FILES_CHANGED, VERIFICATION

unblock_direction.spec:
Unblock '{idea_id}' ({idea_name}): missing spec blocks {blocked_text}.

1. Create one spec file with testable requirements and acceptance commands.
2. Run: python3 scripts/validate_spec_quality.py --file <path>
3. Fix until PASS.

SPEC_PATH: [path]
JUDGE: [python3 scripts/validate_spec_quality.py --file <path>]
VALIDATION: [PASS|FAIL]
unblock_direction.process:
Unblock '{idea_id}' ({idea_name}): missing process blocks {blocked_text}.

1. Read linked spec(s): {spec_hint}.
2. Produce minimal step plan mapped to requirement IDs.
3. Include exact verification command per step.

PROCESS_STEPS: [count]
SPEC_COVERAGE: [requirement IDs]
VERIFICATION_COMMANDS: [commands]
unblock_direction.implementation:
Unblock '{idea_id}' ({idea_name}): missing implementation blocks {blocked_text}.

1. Read spec file scope + acceptance commands.
2. Apply smallest patch in allowed files.
3. Run acceptance commands only; fix until PASS.

FILES_CHANGED: [paths]
TESTS: [passed]/[total]
RESULT: [PASS|FAIL]
unblock_direction.validation:
Unblock '{idea_id}' ({idea_name}): missing validation evidence.

1. Run each spec acceptance command.
2. Record command -> PASS/FAIL.

VALIDATION: [PASS|FAIL]
EVIDENCE: [command -> result list]

idea_progress_direction:
Advance '{idea_id}' ({idea_name}).{gap_hint}

1. Identify the next blocked checkpoint only.
2. Complete exactly one checkpoint with verification evidence.
3. Do not start the next checkpoint.

CHECKPOINT: [completed checkpoint]
EVIDENCE: [judge command + result]
NEXT: [single next checkpoint]
idea_progress_gap_hint: No spec yet — create a spec with 3-5 testable requirements and ROI estimate.
spec_progress_direction:
Spec '{spec_id}' ({title}) chunk {chunk_index}/{chunk_total}: {chunk_label}.
{chunk_goal}
Deliver one chunk only. Stop after one verifiable artifact.

CHUNK_RESULT: [delivered output]
EVIDENCE: [artifact path or command -> PASS]

cli_flow_directions.impl:
Implement per spec.
Spec: {spec_path} | Impl: {impl_path} | Verify: {verify_path}

1. Read {spec_path}; extract assertions.
2. Apply minimal patch in {impl_path}.
3. Ensure {verify_path} checks one assertion per criterion.
4. Run: python3 {verify_path}
5. Fix until all assertions PASS.

VERIFICATION: [output of python3 {verify_path}]
RESULT: [PASS|FAIL]
cli_flow_directions.review:
Review per agreed judge.
Spec: {spec_path} | Impl: {impl_path} | Verify: {verify_path}

1. Read criteria in {spec_path}.
2. Run: python3 {verify_path}
3. For each FAIL, provide minimal patch guidance (file:line:fix).

PASS_FAIL: [PASS|FAIL]
VERIFIED: [passed]/[total]
JUDGE: [python3 {verify_path}]
FINDINGS: [one line per issue, or 'none']
PATCH_GUIDANCE: [file:line:fix per failure, or 'none']
